title: austrodanthonia auriculata
intro: Austrodanthonia auriculata, also known as the ear-flowered danthonia, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of Austrodanthonia auriculata are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of infection. Herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions can be made from the plant to leverage its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
